steps.title: Trace the passage's structure
steps.content: The passage describes two prior studies (Lin et al. and Veldt et al.), then says the gap between them — the "relative prevalence" question — "motivated" Hale and Thorne's 2012 study. The passage is setting up what Hale and Thorne did and why, not making an argument or reporting their findings.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To situate Hale and Thorne's study in the context of their field.’: The passage does exactly this: it shows what was already known, identifies the gap, and explains how that gap led to Hale and Thorne's work. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘To suggest that Hale and Thorne's conclusions would be more persuasive if they incorporated findings from the studies by Lin and colleagues and Veldt and colleagues.’: The passage makes no suggestion that Hale and Thorne should have done anything differently. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To summarize Hale and Thorne's criticism of the study by Lin and colleagues.’: Hale and Thorne are not described as criticizing Lin et al.; the gap is presented as a field-wide open question. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To argue that Hale and Thorne's study revealed a previously unrecognized issue that evolutionary biologists need to address.’: The issue was already recognized; the passage says it was poorly understood before Hale and Thorne studied it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
